headlines.title: Chris Mason: Potential leadership challengers jostle for position
headlines.description: Sir Keir Starmer and his backers argue a leadership contest would paralyse the government for months.

headlines.title: UK economy sees surprise growth in March despite Iran war
headlines.description: The economy grew by 0.3% in the month, official figures show, confounding  analysts' forecasts of a small contraction.

headlines.title: Watch: What happened on day one of Trump's China visit?
headlines.description: China welcomed US President Donald Trump with cheering children and a troop parade on Thursday, before a nearly two-hour long meeting with Xi-Jinping.

headlines.title: Streeting hails NHS progress as key hospital waiting time milestone met
headlines.description: Government his its interim target of 65% of patients in England being treated within 18 weeks.

headlines.title: Madonna, Shakira & BTS to headline World Cup half-time show
headlines.description: Madonna, Shakira and K-pop boy band BTS will headline the Super Bowl-style half-time show at this summer's World Cup final.

headlines.title: Rescuers pull dead from rubble of Kyiv flats after massive Russian strikes
headlines.description: Children are among the injured after Russia launched drones and missiles across Ukraine, officials say.

headlines.title: Met Police prepares armoured vehicles and 4,000 officers for dual London protests
headlines.description: It comes as a Unite the Kingdom rally is taking place in central London on the same day as the annual Nakba march.

headlines.title: Deaths of three women in sea are tragic and horrendous, Brighton council leader says
headlines.description: Brighton & Hove City Council leader Bella Sankey says the women are yet to be identified.

headlines.title: VAR penalty twist sets up Hearts & Celtic showdown for the ages
headlines.description: On a night of bewilderment, there was one unarguable truth. Hearts still have this in their own hands. Only Celtic can stop them, writes Tom English.

headlines.title: At a glance: Starmer fights to stay on as prime minister
headlines.description: The prime minister is fighting to stay on in No 10 as heavy election losses trigger a Labour revolt.

headlines.title: The potential challengers to the PM
headlines.description: Andy Burnham, Angela Rayner and Wes Streeting are all potential candidates for the top job.

headlines.title: How would a Labour leadership contest work?
headlines.description: How could MPs who want to see Sir Keir Starmer replaced force a leadership contest?

headlines.title: Watch: Angela Rayner resolves tax affairs - what now?
headlines.description: BBC chief political correspondent Henry Zeffman explains what this could mean for a potential Labour leadership contest.

headlines.title: 'It's bigger, bolder, bonkier!' Behind the scenes on Rivals season two
headlines.description: Stars including Danny Dyer, Aidan Turner and David Tennant share secrets on the set of the hit show.

headlines.title: I nearly died after buying skinny jab from friend of friend
headlines.description: A woman is warning others of the dangers of unregulated jabs after one dose of a black market pen.

headlines.title: Becoming a mum left me incontinent - we need to talk about birth injuries
headlines.description: Geeta Nayar, who has lived with bowel incontinence since her daughter was born, urges others not to suffer in silence.

headlines.title: What's happening to the UK economy and how does it affect you?
headlines.description: The rate of UK economic growth affects things like pay increases and the amount of tax raised.

headlines.title: Why big tech is betting on cute mascots
headlines.description: The likes of Apple, Microsoft and Google are all putting cartoon characters centre stage.

headlines.title: Hezbollah support endures in south Lebanon as ceasefire fails to stop war with Israel
headlines.description: Despite continuing Israeli attacks and occupation, many people in the south still believe the armed group is the only force capable of defending them.

headlines.title: Harry warns of 'deeply troubling' rise in antisemitism in UK
headlines.description: The Duke of Sussex says "legitimate protest" is important but people must be more "clear" about where they are aiming their anger.

headlines.title: E-gate access to be expanded at UK airports to include younger children
headlines.description: Children aged eight and nine returning to the UK from abroad will be able to use e-gates from 8 July.

headlines.title: Cuba has run out of diesel and oil, energy minister says
headlines.description: The minister says the situation in Cuba is "extremely tense", as a US-led blockade of oil to the country causes widespread power cuts.

headlines.title: HMRC to use AI from British tech firm to spot fraud and tax return errors
headlines.description: Quantexa, a financial data platform, won the £175m contract to spot fraud and tax return errors.

headlines.title: Baby born on Attenborough's 100th birthday named after environmentalist
headlines.description: Stephen Whyte says Sir David has been present in both their lives for as "long as they can remember".

headlines.title: 'I was given a choice - keep my legs or keep my life' - the sepsis patient who lived
headlines.description: The life-threatening reaction to infection caused Marshall Wylie to lose his legs.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Starmer Says He’s Staying
headlines.description: Starmer vows to fight on despite another day of pressure from his party to quit.

headlines.title: Does Trump need China to end the war in Iran?
headlines.description: What you need to know about Donald Trump vs President Xi

headlines.title: A moving portrait of wartime Britain through children’s eyes
headlines.description: Some of the last survivors of the Blitz, children who weren't evacuated, tell their story.

headlines.title: Was it a penalty? Celtic-Hearts title decider set up as McInnes slams 'disgusting' call
headlines.description: Hearts head coach Derek McInnes says his side are "up against everybody" after a "disgusting" 99th-minute penalty winner for Celtic ensured the Scottish Premiership title race goes to a final-day decider on Saturday.

headlines.title: Will F1 go back to the future with its engines?
headlines.description: F1 is in the midst of two parallel sets of engine discussions. Andrew Benson looks at whether one of them will lead to the return of V8s.

headlines.title: Assist of the season? Mesmerising Foden keeps title hopes alive
headlines.description: Phil Foden's magical first-half assist proves the catalyst that Manchester City needed to maintain their challenge in this season's fascinating Premier League title race.

headlines.title: McIlroy 'totally fine' for US PGA after toe issue
headlines.description: World number two Rory McIlroy says using a "bigger and wider shoe with more cushioning" is helping deal with a sore little toe on his right foot.

headlines.title: Hall, 18, becomes youngest to score MLS hat-trick
headlines.description: Forward Julian Hall makes MLS history by becoming the youngest player to score a hat-trick in Red Bull New York's win over Columbus Crew.

headlines.title: An iron fist joining a broken team: Inside Mourinho's Real return
headlines.description: Jose Mourinho is set to be appointed Real Madrid's new manager. Spanish football expert Guillem Balague explains why.
